observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Management consider shifting to Automated Payroll System (APS) for processing and for payment of salaries and wages of JO under to ensure better control over handling of cash, administrative efficiency and mitigate the risk of loss, misappropriation, and other risks associated with manual handling of cash.
observations.managementAction: The JO employees have submitted their online registration and required documents for the opening of their payroll accounts. However, there has been no update from the bank regarding the status of their applications.
observations.reason: There has been no update from the bank regarding the status of their applications. Reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. establish the GAD Database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation; and b. undertake gender analysis based on GAD database to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects, and activities are responsive to the said issues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is still awaiting the result of the data gathering conducted by the Community-Based Monitoring System Committee (CBMC) and Population Census but for the meantime, they are using the collected data from different offices as well as the Record of Barangay Management Information System (RBMIS) as an alternative tool.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still awaiting the result of the data-gathering conducted by the Community-Based Monitoring System Committee (CBMC) and Population Census. Reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to see to it that disbursement vouchers prepared for the remittance of Due to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG accounts covered the total amount of deductions from source; and henceforth, strictly observe compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the remittance of mandatory deductions from salaries and wages of government employees.
observations.managementAction: The MHRMO has made significant efforts to improve the remittance process for employee contributions. They are reviewing outstanding balances with GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and PhilHealth to ensure accurate accounting and timely remittance. The HRMO has maintained communication with these agencies to resolve discrepancies and is implementing new monitoring mechanisms to prevent future issues. Additionally, they continue to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office for efficient and transparent processing. Further, the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer are currently in the process of reconciling the unremitted amounts and updating the accounts of employees pertaining to various statutory deductions.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to:

a. Immediately identify and implement the critical infrastructure components required for the full operational capacity of the Evacuation Center, including establishing a reliable water supply, sewage systems, electricity, and road access, to ensure the center is fully functional during emergencies;

b. Strictly follow the project duration stipulated in the MOA/contract and ensure that future projects are completed within the agreed timelines; and

c. Henceforth, ensure that sites selected for future projects meet all necessary requirements, such as accessibility, infrastructure availability, and other prerequisites, to avoid unforeseen delays and additional costs due to site limitations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Human Resources and Management Officer (MHRMO) to:

a. Coordinate with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) to follow up on the status of the payroll account applications of JO employees; and

b. Adopt the use of an Automated Teller Machine (ATM)-based payroll system for the payment of wages to JO employees to protect the funds of the Municipality from possible risk and to enable more efficient and safer government transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct all the members of GFPS TWG to undergo training on the HGDG tool to build their competence in conducting gender analysis of GAD PPAs for proper GAD Budget attribution; and

b. Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, in coordination with the GFPS, to establish and maintain a GAD Database to serve as the basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Municipal Treasurer to process the application for insurance with the GSIS of all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality; b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Municipality’s annual budget the appropriation for the payment of insurance premiums of all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality; and c. direct the Municipal Accountant to provide necessary assistance to the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality will be covered with insurance in compliance with RA No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office (GSO) personnel have submitted the Consolidated Property Inventory Form (PIF) in support of the application for the Municipality property insurance coverage with the GSIS. The Municipal Budget Officer confirmed that insurance expenses for government assets are always included in the annual budget, and financial reports are submitted annually for data verification. The former Acting Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer coordinated with the GSO Designate to obtain insurance for other buildings not included in the existing coverage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to refrain from appropriating and charging the above-cited expenses which are not allowed to be charged under LDRRMF and do not support disaster risk reduction activities.
observations.managementAction: Management ensured compliance with the applicable provisions and incurred expenses strictly in accordance with the allowable uses of the LDRRMF for CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: a) see to it that revision of real property assessments be made every three years to update the fair market value of taxable properties and derive additional income therefrom; and b) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Depends upon the issuance of Sangguniang Panlalawigan Resolution on the revision of real property tax assessments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• direct the Municipal Treasurer to process the application for insurance
with the GSIS of all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality
following the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2018-002;
• instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Municipality’s
annual budget the appropriation for the payment of insurance premiums
of all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality; and
• direct the Municipal Accountant to provide necessary assistance to the
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all insurable assets and properties of
the Municipality will be covered with insurance in compliance with RA
No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Management consider shifting to APS
for processing and payment of salaries and wages of personnel under Job Order to ensure better control over handling of cash, administrative
efficiency and mitigate the risk of loss, misapporopriation, and other risks
associated with manual handling of cash.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer and MDRRMO to refrain from appropriating and charging the above-
cited expenses which are not allowed to be charged under LDRRMF and do
not support disaster risk management activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person
to:
• establish the GAD database with adequate and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender
analysis, planning, programming, and policy formulation; and
• undertake gender analysis based on GAD database to identify and
confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects,
and activities are responsive to the said issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers prepared for the remittance of Due to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG accounts covered the total amount of deductions from source; and
Henceforth, strictly observe compliance with pertinent laws, rules and
regulations on mandatory deductions from salaries of government
employees and remittance to the concerned agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting-Human Resource Management Officer to: a) prepare the contract of services, MOA, and job order between the Municipality of Luisiana and the individual; b) submit the contract of services, MOA, and job order to the CSCRO for review; and c) strictly adhere to the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 regarding policy guidelines for contract of services, MOA and job order.
observations.managementAction:
The contract of services, MOA, and job order between the Municipality of Luisiana and the individual was already prepared.
The submission of Job Orders to CSCRO for review; review was repealed by CSC Resolution No. 06-2254 and circularized by CSC Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 01, series of 2007.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and the Municipal Engineer to: a) address the cause/s for the delay and/or non- implementation of the projects; b) maximize the utilization of the 20 percent DF budget to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality; and c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1.
observations.managementAction:
a) address the cause/s for the delay and/or non- implementation of the projects; The causes of delays/non- implementation of the project were already addressed in CY 2023.

b) maximize the utilization of the 20 percent DF budget with to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and The fund was optimally utilized with utilization rate of 98.18 percent.

c) henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Muncipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to: a) adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP for effective utilization of LDRRMF; and b) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of Section 21 of RA no. 10121 and the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction:
a) adequately plan and monitor the PPAs listed in the LDRRMFIP for effective utilization of LDRRMF; The fund was optimally utilized with utilization rate of 77.41 percent and remaining two PPAs with ongoing implementation.

b) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of Section 21 of RA no. 10121 and the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
observations.reason: Some PPAs included in the appropriation of the fund were not chargeable against the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the General Services Officer to:
a) coordinate with the Accounting Office to reconcile their property records and to
ascertain the cause of the total discrepancy;
b) ensure that all the relevant information such as Property Number, Year
Acquired, Location and Remarks on the condition of the Property are disclosed
in the RPCPPE;
c) henceforth, strictly observe compliance with Section 124 0f the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a) revert to the unappropriated surplus the accounts payables of more than
two years whose claims are no longer valid;
b) review and monitor the balances of accounts payable to determine those
claims that are no longer valid; and
c) henceforth, strictly comply with Section 98 of PD No. 1445 on reversion of
long outstanding payables.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City
Accountant to:
a) disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial
Statements in the ensuing year to assist the users in understanding the
financial statements;
b) henceforth, strictly comply with Paragraph 1 of IPSAS 2 and Paragraphs 127
and 129 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer/General Services Officer to:
a. create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake
an appraisal of these unserviceable property and prepare a
corresponding report thereon;
b. submit to the Office of the Auditor a request for disposal of all
unserviceable property together with the Inventory and Inspection
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), Appraisal Report, and other
required documents for appropriate action once the appraisal has been
conducted and the documents required are complete; and
c. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 79 of
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Section 125 of the Manual on the
New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government
Units (LGUs), Volume I.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO
to:
a. ensure that the LDRRMF is strictly appropriated and utilized for the
implementation of PPAs related to disaster prevention and mitigation,
preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery;
b. continue to strategize on the implementation of the Municipality’s Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP),
despite limitation and challenges imposed by overlapping crises; and
c. henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
• prepare and submit a RPCPPE, with complete details with regard to the year acquired and whereabouts and condition of the specified.
• provide property tags to all PPEs as part of internal control and proper identification purposes;
• ensure that all PPEs AREs are supported with Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), which is renewable every three years or whenever there is change in accountability; and
• prepare and maintain property cards for each PPE category and reconcile the balance with the physical inventory reported in the RPCPPE.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE ledger card for each class of PPE to support the balance appearing in the general or control accounts and reconcile the same with the physical inventory reported in the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: RPCPPE was properly submitted; however, details as to Property Number, location, year acquired and whereabouts and condition were not specified. Property tags of PPEs were provided but not specified in the RPCPPE. AREs were issued to end-users and renewed every three years. No action was undertaken by the Management. Property cards for all PPEs were not maintained due to manpower constraint. Municipal Accountant provided excel worksheets to record the details of all the PPEs of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Lack of complete details regarding the PPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop the small value property items from the books of GF and Special Education Fund by debiting Prior Period Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation and crediting the respective PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment for the value property items from the books of GF and SEF are adjusted on April 2021.

Road map was not yet submitted. The Municipal Engineer provided the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects. Inventory Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network was submitted. The Municipal Accountant provided a separate disclosure of the Road Networks account in the Notes to Financial Statement using the prescribed format. The Municipal Accountant provided the Local Road Network Ledger Card.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of labelling the road.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to:
• refrain from the practice of directly charging the purchase of supplies to the corresponding supplies expense accounts, instead, use the perpetual inventory method in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
• maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies to record all receipts and issuances, and reconcile the balance with the control accounts and physical inventory of supplies.
b) Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO to :
• prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of supplies to record all receipts and issuances, and reconcile the same with the physical inventory reported in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and
• prepare and submit semestrally a properly accomplished RPCI.
observations.managementAction: Supplies Ledger Cards were maintained and reconciled with the physical inventory of supplies. Maintenance of stock cards for each type of supplies was on-going. Stock cards for each type of supplies was on-going. No action was undertaken by the Management for the semestral RPCI. Manpower constraint because the Municipal Treasurer is also the GSO.
observations.reason: Manpower constraint.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• submit lacking the documents for the mentioned disbursements for further verification by the Audit Team;
• refrain from recognizing as outright expense those items classified as inventory, instead follow the recording and reporting procedures provided under Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required reports for proper monitoring and reporting of the receipts and issuances of inventories and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper amount of inventory that will be recognized as expense for a period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the lacking documents on July 8, 2021. The Municipal Accountant followed the recording and reporting procedures provided under Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I. The Acting Municipal Treasurer submitted the required reports for proper monitoring and reporting of the receipts and issuances of inventories and coordinated with the Municipal Accountant for the proper amount of inventory that will be recognized as expense for a period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to:
a. ensure that the LDRRMF is strictly appropriated and utilized for the implementation of PPAs related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery;
b. continue to strategize on the implementation of the Municipality’s Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP), despite limitation and challenges imposed by overlapping crises; and
c. endeavour to ensure PPAs for the substantial/full implementation of future LDRRMF programs to benefit the constituents in compliance with the purpose of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMF is Fully appropriated and utilized for the implementation of PPAs related to disaster prevention and mitigation and rehabilitation and recovery.
observations.reason: Some projects were not implemented due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Development Council to:
• continue its commitment in implementing the projects under the 20 percent DF in order that the desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes will be continually achieved; and
• include only in the appropriation for the 20 percent DF the development projects listed under Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Development projects for CY 2021 were not fully implemented due to restrictions of the Covid 19 pandemic. PPAs for CY 2021 were not fully implemented.
observations.reason: PPAs were not fully implemented due to the restrictions of the Covid 19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to fully implement the PPAs in coordination with the other City officials for the benefits and protection of children and specify the programs included in the LCPC budget in compliance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.
observations.reason: PPAs were not fully implemented due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal DRRM Officer to:
 a. refrain the charging of expenses not attributable to the implementation of the DRRM programs of the Municipality in compliance with Items 4.3 and 4.4 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; and
 b. prepare an annual LDRRMFIP and include under a separate caption the programs and activities that will be funded from the previous years' unexpended LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Unexpended LDRRMF in the Trust Fund were treated as supplemental fund and will be used as needed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
 conduct actual physical count of all PPEs owned by the Municipality in order to
ascertain their condition and whereabouts;
 prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE;
 provide property tags to all PPEs as part of internal control and for proper
identification purposes; and
 ensure that all PPEs issued to end-users are supported with ARE, which is
renewable every three years or whenever there is change in accountability.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to determine the source of the noted
discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments in order to reconcile their
records. In addition, prepare and maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards
for each class of PPE and reconcile the same with the physical inventory
reported in the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop the small value property items from
the books of the General Fund and Special Education Fund by debiting Prior
Period Adjustment and Accumulated Depreciation of the respective PPE
accounts and crediting the respective PPE accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
 General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the
Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate
the proper reporting and preparation of inventory of its Road Networks, and
maintain the required Local Road Network Property Card;
 Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General
Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
 Inventory Committee to prepare and submit yearly a separate Report on the
Physical Count of Local Road Network; and
 Municipal Accountant to provide disclosure of the Road Networks account in
the Notes to the Financial Statement using the prescribed format and
maintain the required Local Road Network Ledger Card.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
 refrain from the practice of directly charging the purchase of supplies to
the corresponding expense accounts, instead, use the perpetual
inventory method in recording, in compliance with Section 114 of the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
 use the SSMI that will be submitted by the Municipal Treasurer as the
basis for recording the amount of supplies and materials that will be
recognized as expense during a period; and
 maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies to record all
receipts and issuances, and reconcile the balance with the control
accounts and physical inventory of supplies.
b. Municipal Treasurer to:
 prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the SSMI, which will be
the basis for recording of the inventory that will be expensed in a period;
 prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of supplies to record all
receipts and issuances, and reconcile the same with the physical
inventory reported in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI); and
 prepare and submit semestrally a properly accomplished RPCI.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
 submit immediately the lacking documents for the mentioned
disbursements for further verification by the Audit Team;
 refrain from recognizing as outright expense those items classified as
inventory, instead follow the recording and reporting procedures provided
under Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Acting Municipal Treasurer to maintain the required reports for proper
monitoring and reporting of the receipts and issuances of inventories and
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the proper amount of inventory
that will be recognized as expense for a period;
c. Concerned officials to comply with the posting requirement for information
and transparency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to:
a. ensure that the LDRRMF is strictly appropriated and utilized for the
implementation of PPAs related to disaster prevention and mitigation,
preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery;
b. continue to strategize on the implementation of the Municipality’s Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP),
despite limitation and challenges imposed by overlapping crises; and
c. endeavour to ensure substantial/full implementation of future LDRRMF
programs to benefit the constituents in compliance with the purpose of RA
No. 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Development
Council to:
 continue its commitment in implementing the development projects under
the 20 percent DF in order that the desired socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes will be continually achieved; and
 include only in the appropriation for the 20 percent DF the allowable
development projects listed under Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare
and Development Officer (MSWDO) to fully implement the PPAs in coordination
with the other City officials for the benefits and protection of children and
specify the programs included in the LCPC budget in compliance with Section
15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to:
 prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE, with complete details with regard to the whereabouts and condition of the PPEs;
 provide property tags to all PPEs as part of internal control and for proper identification purposes; and
 ensure that all PPEs issued to end-users are supported with Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), which is renewable every three years or whenever there is change in accountability.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to determine the source of the noted discrepancy and make the necessary adjustments in order to reconcile their records. In addition, prepare and maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards for each class of PPE and reconcile the same with the physical inventory reported in the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer was able to submit the RPCPPE for CY 2020. However, it still lacks information with regard to the whereabouts and condition of several PPEs.
The Accounting Office used alternative method in monitoring the PPEs of the Municipality. The Office used excel worksheets to record the details of all the PPEs of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Manpower constraint since the Municipal Treasurer was also the designated General Services Officer (GSO).
No action was undertaken by the Management.
The Municipal Treasurer overlooked the purchase of property tags.
Renewal/Updating of the issued AREs were not undertaken due to manpower constraint.
Property cards for all PPEs were not maintained due to manpower constraint.

observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management.
observations.reason: The details of the remaining Road Networks already have their respective property cards. Verification of the recorded Road Networks is in process in order to provide the complete disclosure.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
 a. Municipal Accountant to:
  refrain from the practice of directly charging the purchase of supplies to the corresponding expense accounts, instead, use the perpetual inventory method in recording, in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
  use the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) that will be submitted by the Municipal Treasurer as the basis for recording the amount of supplies and materials that will be recognized as expense during a period; and
  maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies to record all receipts and issuances, and reconcile the balance with the control accounts and physical inventory of supplies.
 b. Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO to:
  prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the SSMI, which will be the basis for recording of the inventory that will be expensed in a period;
  prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of supplies to record all receipts and issuances, and reconcile the same with the physical inventory reported in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and
  prepare and submit semestrally a properly accomplished RPCI.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer commited to implement the recommendations in the succeeding year.
observations.reason:
Manpower constraint since the Municipal Treasurer was also the designated General Services Officer (GSO).
No action was undertaken by the Management.
Supplies Ledger Cards were not maintained due to lack of time and voluminous work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer/Designated General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of subject lots as well as the transfer of titles still in process to avoid contingent claims and disputes that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 47 of the IRR of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality have started the processing of the transfer of titles of the acquired lots.
observations.reason: The process of transfer of titles takes a lot of time since the Municipality did not hire the services of professionals to avoid high cost for service fee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the measures and strategies to intensify collection of taxes and collection of taxes. Likewise, avail of the remedies and sanctions provided under Sections 256 and 258 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes.
observations.reason: Collection of RPT/SET taxes was not strictly imposed due to pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat to examine the validity period posted by every winning contractor in order to ensure that this complies with the requirement that it shall be valid until the end of the defects liability period.
observations.managementAction: The performance security submitted by the contractors for the recent projects covered the whole implementation period of the projects and upon completion, the contractors are required to submit a warranty security to cover any structural defects/failure that may occur.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
 a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
  ensure that the Installation of Water Supply to Schools project will be immediately implemented once the cited Ulbok Spring project will serve as the source of the water for the project and will be completed in order for the intended beneficiaries to have access to clean and potable water supply; and
  follow-up from the DepEd its purchased lateral drawers, instead of monoblock chairs, as approved by DepEd.
 b) Municipal Engineer to:
  consistently monitor the implementation of the Potable Water System project to ensure that it will be completed within the revised target date of completion in order for the intended beneficiaries to have access to clean and potable water supply; and
  start the implementation of the Construction of Slaughterhouse project in the ensuing year.
 c) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to continue providing financial assistance to qualified indigent individual or family to help these people in alleviating their financial needs.
observations.managementAction:
a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
 ensure that the Installation of Water Supply to Schools project will be immediately implemented once the cited Ulbok Spring project will serve as the source of the water for the project and will be completed in order for the intended beneficiaries to have access to clean and potable water supply; and
 follow-up from the DepEd its purchased lateral drawers, instead of monoblock chairs, as approved by DepEd.
b) Municipal Engineer to:
 consistently monitor the implementation of the Potable Water System project to ensure that it will be completed within the revised target date of completion in order for the intended beneficiaries to have access to clean and potable water supply; and
 start the implementation of the Construction of Slaughterhouse project in the ensuing year.
c) Municipal Social Financial assistance to continue providing financial assistance to qualified indigent individual or family to help these people in alleviating their financial needs.
This project was completed on October 4, 2020.
This project is currently ongoing with a cost of P7,654,732.00.
Financial assistance totalling P4,999,096.13 was given to 2,030 beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council to:
 continue its commitment in implementing the development projects under the 20 percent DF in order that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes will be continually achieved; and
 include only in the appropriation for the 20 percent DF the allowable development projects listed under Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator guaranteed that only allowable development projects provided under the JMC will be charged against the 20 percent Development Fund in the succeeding year.
observations.reason: Disbursements charged against the 20 percent DF for CY 2020 were reduced from 95.46 percent in CY 2019 to 71.58 percent in CY 2020 because of Covid-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to continue its good practice of immediately implementing the programs, projects and activities embodied in the LDRRM Plan in order that higher level of disaster preparedness and responsiveness will be maintained and the community will be protected from disasters.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO guaranteed compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Other programs, projects and activities were not implemented due to the limitations brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to continue the good practice of implementing the GAD programs, projects and activities listed in the GAD Plan and Budget in order that the goal of women’s empowerment and gender equality will be maintained in the locality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has utilized P3,480,830.26 or 83.81 percent of the GAD fund of P4,153,126.30 for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to ensure that the annual GAD Plan and Budget will be submitted to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed period in order that it will be reviewed and then endorsed.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality for CY 2020 was submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the proper disclosure of the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements by the Municipal Accountant and proper maintenance of Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card by the GSO.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Management.
observations.reason: Compliance with the recommendation was not undertaken due to lack of coordination between the concerned agency officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO to maintain Stock Cards and conduct actual count of its Inventory accounts and prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team as required by Sections 119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by the Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower constraint.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer adopt measures and strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate more income. Coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of Real Property Tax Program to maximize collection of taxes. Likewise, avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed to comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Compliance with the recommendation was not undertaken due to manpower constraint.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
• prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE, with complete details with
regard to the whereabouts and condition of the PPEs;
• provide property tags to all PPEs as part of internal control and for proper
identification purposes;
• ensure that all PPEs issued to end-users are supported with Acknowledgement
Receipt for Equipment, which is renewable every three years or whenever there is
change in accountability; and
• prepare and maintain property cards for each PPE category and reconcile the
balance with the physical inventory reported in the RPCPPE.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE ledger card for each class of PPE to
support the balance appearing in the general or control accounts and reconcile
the same with the physical inventory reported in the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) General Services Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to
prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the proper reporting
and preparation of inventory of its Road Networks, and maintain the
required Local Road Network Property Card;
b) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General
Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects;
c) Inventory Committee to prepare and submit yearly a separate Report on the
Physical Count of Local Road Network; and
d) Municipal Accountant to provide a separate disclosure of the Road
Networks account in the Notes to Financial Statement using the prescribed
format and maintain the required Local Road Network Ledger Card.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Accountant to:
• refrain from the practice of directly charging the purchase of supplies to the
corresponding expense accounts, instead, use the perpetual inventory
method in recording, in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I; and
• maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each type of supplies to record all
receipts and issuances, and reconcile the balance with the control accounts
and physical inventory of supplies.
Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO to :
• prepare and maintain stock cards for each type of supplies to record all
receipts and issuances, and reconcile the same with the physical inventory
reported in the RPCI; and
• prepare and submit semestrally a properly accomplished RPCI.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer/
Designated General Services Officer to facilitate the titling of subject lots as
well as the transfer of title still in process to avoid contingent claims and
disputes that may arise in the future in compliance with Section 148 of COA
Circular No. 92-386 and Section 47 of the IRR of the Local Government Code of
1991.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
adopt measures and strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate
more income for the Municipality. Likewise, avail of the remedies and sanctions
provided under RA No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to
examine the validity period indicated in the performance security posted by
every winning contractor in order to ensure that this complies with the
requirement that it shall be valid until the end of the defects liability period
since this will serve as the Municipality’s guaranty that the contractor will
perform his responsibilities during such period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
• ensure that the Installation of Water Supply to Schools project will be
immediately implemented once the cited Ulbok Spring Development
project was completed in order for the intended beneficiaries to have
access to clean and potable water supply; and
• follow-up from the DepEd its response to the Municipality’s proposal for the
conversion of the wooden chairs to monobloc chairs.
b) Municipal Engineer to:
• consistently monitor the implementation of the Potable Water System
project to ensure that it will be completed within the revised target date
of completion in order for the intended beneficiaries to have access to
clean and potable water supply; and
• start the implementation of the Construction of Slaughterhouse project
in the ensuing year.
c) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to continue providing
financial assistance to qualified indigent individual or family to help these
people in alleviating their financial needs.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the RPCPPE for CY 2019. However, PPEs were not provided with property tags. The Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO was able to submit the RPCPPE for CY 2019. However, PPEs were not provided with property tags. The Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO was able to submit the RPCPPE for CY 2019. However, PPEs were not provided with property tags. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer gave assurance that they will exert extra effort to fully comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Manpower constraint since the Municipal Treasurer was also the GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal personnel in-charge to facilitate the transfer of the titles of the acquired lands in the name of the Municipality to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality have started the processing of the transfer of titles of the acquired lots. The Municipal Treasurer explained that since they did not hire the services of professionals, it will take time before all the titles for the mentioned parcel of lots will be transferred in the name of the Municipality. Nevertheless, she gave assurance that her office is continuously going through the process in order to fully comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason: No additional lot was transferred in the name of the Municipality due to its lengthy process since they did not hire the services of professionals in order to save money for the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Members submit the required documents in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the required supporting documents on May 30, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the full utilization and implementation of the planned projects for the 20 per cent Development Fund so that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents of the Municipality and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. We also recommended that regular monitoring mechanisms be employed in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation and that a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to ensure that programmed projects are implemented and completed on time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to optimally utilize the 20 per cent Development Fund, the Management gave assurance that they are committed in immediately implementing all the development projects for the benefit of its constituents. Furthermore, they guarantee that no more maintenance and other operating expenses will be charged against the 20 per cent DF in the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen and implementation of the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the LCPC, pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: The LCPC fund amounting to P773,672.76 for CY 2019 was optimally utilized by the Municipality, the Management gave assurance that they will continue to implement the programs, projects and activities of LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to submit the required attachments of the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report, as provided under Section 4.1.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, such as: (a) brief summary of the reported program or project; (b) copies of reported policy issuances; (c) results of HGDG tests, if any; and (d) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted the applicable attachments.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, thru its LGU GAD Focal Point System, fully utilize the GAD Budget and implement the GAD planned activities to promote gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction: Of the total GAD fund of the Municipality, the Municipality was able to implement the GAD planned activities; the Management appreciate the positive feedback on their accomplishment report on GAD and gave assurance that they will continue the good practice of immediately implementing all the programs, projects and activities under the GAD fund for the benefit of its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to comply with DILG MC No. 2012-73 and ensure that programs, projects and activities embodied in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan are implemented to protect the community from disasters.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality optimally utilized the 70 percent Mitigation Fund amounting to P3,276,027.13 of the LDRRMF for CY 2019, utilizing P3,071,498.62 or 93.76 per cent thereof. The Management gave assurance that they are committed in immediately implementing all the programs, projects and activities under the LDRRMF for the benefit of its constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute the MSWMB membership by filling-up the position of the Chairperson of the SK Federation and include a representative from the manufacturing or packaging industry to conform with the requirements of Section 5, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has reconstituted the membership of the MSWMB to include the SK Federation and representative from the manufacturing or packaging industry as additional members.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor encourage and support the establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperative or Association that will help promote implementation of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the MENRO to inform its constituents about the importance of having a Multi-Purpose Environment Cooperative or Association.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal Official and Municipal Accountant to submit copy of the MOA or Contract Agreement and all supporting documents pertaining the garbage disposal to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Lacking supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Barangay Officials to look for conducive locations for the MRFs for all its barangays, in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 and DILG MC No. 2009-168.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO confirmed that as of year-end of CY 2019, all barangays under his jurisdiction have already established the required MRF.

observations.recommendation:
We reiteratedour recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the MGSOto:
∑ conduct physical count of property and provide property tags on all PPE
owned by the Municipality;
∑ prepare and submit a properly accomplished RPCPPE;
∑ prepare and maintain PCs for each PPE category to support the balance
appearing in the general/controlling account;and
∑ issue ARE to end-users of issued PPEs and renew them every three years or
whenever there is change in accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the proper
disclosure of the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements by the
Municipal Accountant and proper maintenance of Local Road Inventory, Road
Map and Local Road Network Property Card by the MGSO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General
Services Officer (MGSO) to maintain Stock Cards and conduct actual count of its
Inventory accounts and prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team as
required by Sections 119 and 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal personnel in-
charge to facilitate the transfer of titles of the acquired lands in the name of the
Municipal Government of Luisiana to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
adopt measures and strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate more
income. Coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of Real Property
Tax Program to maximize collection of taxes. Likewise, avail of remedies and
sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee Members submit the
required documents, in compliance with Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the full utilization and
implementation of the planned projects for the 20 per cent Development Fund so
that the desired services and benefits can be delivered to the constituents of the
Municipality and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes.
We also recommended that regular monitoring mechanisms be employed in
assessing the rate of progress of project implementation and that a closer
coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring
be undertaken to ensure that programmed projects are implemented and
completed on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MCPC to fully utilize the
amount of one percent of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the
programs, projects and activities of the LCPC, pursuant to Section 15 of RA No.
9344.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: ∑ prepare and submit a properly accomplished Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); ∑ prepare and maintain Property Cards for each PPE category of the Municipality to support the balance appearing in the general/controlling account; ∑ conduct physical count of property and provide property tag on all PPE owned by the Municipality; and ∑ issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to end-users and renew all AREs every three years or whenever there is a change in accountability.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO committed to properly accomplish the RPCPPE, complete the issuance of ARE and tagging of all the properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Conditions of the property were not indicated

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Treasurer/General Services Officer and Assessor to reconcile the discrepancies between their records. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator, Assessor and GSO to facilitate the transfer of lots in the name of the Municipal Government of Luisiana to support claims of ownership and to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The records maintained for the Land account by the Municipal Accountant, Assessor and Treasurer were already reconciled. The preparation and maintenance of Property Cards were almost complete. Titling of lands was still in process.
observations.reason: Titling of lands was still in process. No lots were titled for the year.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: ∑ prepare the necessary documents for the disposal of the unserviceable property; ∑ request the disposal of the unserviceable property; and ∑ dispose the unserviceable property upon approval of the request. In addition, require the Municipal Accountant to drop the disposed property from the Municipality’s book of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The necessary documents for the disposal were submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Request for disposal of the unserviceable properties were submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Disposal has yet to be undertaken. The property have not yet been dropped from the records.
observations.reason: Awaiting for COA TAS Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to: ∑ appropriate at least one percent of the Municipality’s IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the LCPC, pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344; and ∑ separate the LCPC appropriation from programs appropriation for effective monitoring of the implementation of the programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer appropriated one percent of the Municipality’s IRA for LCPC. The LCPC appropriation was separated from GAD appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman to submit the missing documents; and instruct the concerned officials to explain/submit/rectify the deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking supporting documents on May 30, 2019. Explanations for the deficiencies were submitted.
observations.reason: The comments for the deficiencies shall be reviewed and verified by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by availing of the remedies provided under Sections 256 and 258 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Assessor and Treasurer to review the list of taxpayers and prepare proposal on how to increase the collections.
observations.reason: Tax delinquencies decreased to P7,645,742.69. Tax Delinquency was not fully eliminated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly follow the provision of the Joint Circular in the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund to ensure that only projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental undertakings will be charged to the fund. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging disbursement that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures against the 20 per cent DF.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to maintain the GAD database using its data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources such as surveys and studies to ensure reliable source of information for GAD planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: GAD Database was maintained.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality thru its LGU GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) fully utilize the GAD Budget and implement the GAD planned activities to promote gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person assured the full implementation of GAD programs and activities in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: GAD Fund was not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality, thru its MDRRMC, fully utilize the Mitigation Fund/preparedness budget for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF).
observations.managementAction: Utilization for LDRRMF was not fully utilized.
observations.reason: The LDRRM Fund was not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MGSO to: - prepare the necessary documents for the disposal of the unserviceable properties; - request the disposal of the unserviceable properties; and - dispose the unserviceable properties upon approval of the request. In addition, after the disposal, require the Municipal Accountant to drop the disposed properties from the Municipality’s book of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management is still preparing the necessary documents for the disposal.
observations.reason: Documents for the necessary disposal were not submitted. No request for disposal prepared. Unserviceable properties were not disposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:  Appropriate at least 1% of the Municipality’s IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the LCPC pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344; and  Separate the appropriation for LCPC from appropriation for GAD for effective monitoring of the implementation of the programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer assured the Audit Team that they already appropriated one percent of IRA for LCPC for CY 2018.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not allocate 1% of its IRA to LCPC. The appropriation for LCPC was included in GAD Fund instead of separate appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Treasurer to:
 prepare and submit a properly accomplished Report of Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);

 prepare and maintain Property Cards for each PPE category of the
Municipality to support the balance appearing in the general/controlling
account;

 conduct physical count of properties and provide property tag on all
property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality; and

 issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to end-users of
issued PPEs and renew all AREs every three (3) years or whenever there
is a change in accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation that that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services Officer and
Assessor to reconcile the discrepancies between their records on the land
owned by the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Administrator, Assessor and GSO to facilitate the transfer of lots in the name
of the Municipal Government of Luisiana to support claims of ownership
and to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the MGSO to:
- prepare the necessary documents for the disposal of the unserviceable
properties;
- request the disposal of the unserviceable properties; and
- dispose the unserviceable properties upon approval of the request.

In addition, after the disposal, require the Municipal Accountant to
drop the disposed properties from the Municipality’s book of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Budget Officer to:
 appropriate at least one percent of the Municipality’s IRA for the
strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and
activities (PPAs) of the LCPC pursuant to Section 15 of RA No. 9344; and

 separate the appropriation for LCPC from appropriation for GAD for
effective monitoring of the implementation of the programs, projects and
activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. instruct the BAC Chairman to submit the missing documents stated
under No. 1 above; and
2. instruct the concerned officials to explain/submit/rectify the deficiencies
noted under No. 2 above.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to:  prepare and maintain PPELCs and PCs for each PPE category of the Municipality to support the balance appearing in the general/controlling account;  issue Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) to end-users of issued PPEs and renew all AREs every three (3) years or whenever there is a change in accountability;  conduct physical count of properties and provide property tag on all property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality;  prepare and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); and  reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained PPELCs while the Municipal Treasurer started preparing PCs for each PPE category. The Municipal Treasurer submitted the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: PCs were not yet complete. PPELCs prepared and maintained. The conduct of physical count in all Municipal offices was not yet completed. The submitted RPCPPE was not properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services Officer and Assessor to reconcile the discrepancies between their records on the land owned by the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to include in the Annual Budget the allocation for the expenses the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality of Luisiana.
observations.managementAction: The Management is now undergoing reconciliation of their reports. The Municipal Treasurer included the allocation for expenses of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality in the Annual Budget.
observations.reason: Submitted reports were not reconciled. The allocation of expenses was included in CY 2018 Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise all the concerned officials to refund the amount of P 52,100.00 as payment of membership fees and strictly observe the provisions of R.A No. 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 dated April 22, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials refunded the amount due from them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the officials concerned to stop the practice of payment for waste disposal out of the cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer. Checks should be drawn and endorsed directly to whom payment is due.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required documents to the Auditor for evaluation of the validity and propriety of the disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by availing the remedies provided under Sections 256 and 258 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Management is now looking for strategies to improve their tax collection efficiency.
observations.reason: Delinquent taxes remained uncollected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly follow the provision of the Joint Circular in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that only projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management undertakings will be charged to the fund. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging disbursement that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures against the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still incurred expenses that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures. The Management tried to fully utilize the Mitigation Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipality still incurred expenses that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures. Reiterated in audit observation No. 7 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to maintain the GAD database for the Municipality using its data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources such as surveys and studies to ensure reliable source of information for GAD planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The management informed that they are now starting the basic foundation for the establishment of GAD database for the Municipality thru the integration of its 4Ps data and result of its Community-Based Monitoring System data gathering.
observations.reason: GAD database is still being prepared by the GAD Focal Point Person.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, thru its MDRRMC, fully utilize the Mitigation Fund/preparedness budget for the implementation of LDRRMF programs, projects and activities to better prepare and lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: The Management tried to fully utilize the Mitigation Fund. The Municipal DRRM Officer promised to study and prepare an attainable LDRRMF plan and budget to ensure full utilization and implementation of LDRRMF programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: The Management increased the utilization to 51.68%. Reiterated in audit observation No. 10 under Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the desired documents to the Auditor for evaluation of the validity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required documents to the Auditor for evaluation of the validity and propriety of the disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor explain/justify in writing why the implementation of projects/programs contrary to the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 8, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services Officer and Assessor to reconcile the discrepancies between their records on the land owned by the Municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to include in the Annual Budget the allocation for expenses the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality of Luisiana.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, to avoid the further deterioration of the unserviceable properties, direct the MGSO to:
-	prepare the necessary documents for the disposal of the unserviceable properties;
-request the disposal of the unserviceable properties; and
-dispose the unserviceable properties upon approval of the request.
In addition, after the disposal, require the Municipal Accountant to drop the disposed properties from the Municipal’s book of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:
Appropriate at least 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the Local Council for the Protection of Children pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344; and
Separate the Appropriation for LCPC from appropriation for GAD for effective monitoring of the implementation of the programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise all the concerned officials to refund the amount of P52,100.00 as payment of membership fees and annual dues personal and individual to private association/organization, and strictly observe the provisions of R.A 7160 and National Budget Circular No. 563 dated April 22, 2016.

We recommended that the Mayor instruct the officials concerned to stop the practice of payment for waste disposal out of the cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer.  Checks should be drawn and endorsed directly to whom payment is due.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by availing the remedies provided under Section 256 and 258 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly follow the provision of the joint circular in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that only projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management undertakings will be charged to the fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging disbursement that do not partake the nature of capital expenditures against the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point Person to maintain the GAD database for the Municipality using its data-gathering tools and management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and secondary sources such as surveys and studies to ensure reliable source of information for GAD planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Municipality thru its MDRRMC to fully utilize the Mitigation Fund/preparedness budget for the implementation LDRRMF programs, projects and activities to better prepare and lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards and disasters.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the foregoing desired documents to the Auditor for evaluation of the validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a.  Municipal Accountant: - to record purchases of office supplies, non-accountable forms, agriculture and marine supplies, and other supplies as debit to appropriate asset account using the perpetual inventory method, regardless of whether or not they were consumed within the accounting period; - to maintain the subsidiary ledger cards for each supplies and materials; and - to record the issuances base on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). b. Supply Officer: - to maintain stock cards for supplies; - to prepare and submit monthly the RSMI to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of issuances; and - to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory as of a given date.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents supporting the disbursements.

We recommended that the Local School Board Officials strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160, DBM Local Budget Circular No. 62 and DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 relating to the defined usage or utilization of the SEF. Likewise, comply with the provisions of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. of 1998 in identifying projects/programs and activities chargeable against SEF where the budget should reflect an equitable distribution of SEF mandated priority projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • effect the necessary adjustments/journal entries in the books of account to present the correct balance of the Due to Other NGAs account in the General and Trust Funds financial statements; • remit/revert all unexpended/dormant balances, especially the PDAF, to the source agency/general fund or Bureau of Treasury so that it can help augment the finances for other priority projects/programs of the national government or other agencies.
observations.managementAction:
The amount of P211,376.44 refers to BUB fund with on-going projects and adjustments were duly effect ed in the books. Due to Other NGAs
The amount of P42,852.71 was returned to the Bureau of Treasury per Check no. 1493697 dated December 29, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • exert effort to verify the Source Agency of the various receipts; • remit/revert all unexpended/dormant balances to the source agency/general fund or Bureau of Treasury so that it can help augment the finances for other priority projects/programs of the national government or other agencies; and • effect the necessary adjustments/journal entries in the books of account.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱89,788.97 was returned/remitted to the Bureau of Treasury per Check No. 1571010 dated December 29, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the balance of the petty cash fund at the end of the year be returned and a new fund be set up at the ensuing year to avoid exposing government funds to possible misuse/misappropriation and fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer fully liquidated her Petty Cash Fund at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Development Council and in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator allocate the 20% Development Fund and maximize its utilization for developmental projects that shall contribute to the delivery of the desired services and benefits to the constituents of the Municipality and attainment of the desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor explain/justify in writing why the implementation of projects/programs contrary to the provisions of the Joint Memorandum Circular should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: 20% DF for CY 2016 was already fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen the MDRRMC by completing the required membership of the Council.  In addition, include the names of the Division Head/Superintendent of Schools of the DepEd, the highest ranking officer of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) assigned in the area, the Provincial Director/City/Municipal Chief of the Philippine National Police (PNP), the Provincial Director/City/Municipal Fire Marshall of the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP), and the Philippine National Red Cross (PNRC) representatives to fully comply with the provision of Sec. 11 of RA No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC was strengthened and the required membership was completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and prepare and submit all required reports/documents in the prescribed format within the time frame.
observations.managementAction: Required LDRRMF reports were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor strengthen the LGU GFPS by completing the composition of the GFPS Execom and TWG to ensure that the LGU GFPS could fulfill its tasks.
observations.managementAction: The membership of the Municipal Government’s Gender and Development (GAD) Focal Point System System was completed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation of the establishment of the GAD Database that will strengthen the planning and budgeting processes for GAD and ensure that planned PPAs address identified gender issues, that objectives, performance indicators and targets are well-formulated to facilitate monitoring and evaluation for the attainment of GAD objectives.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality started the establishment of GAD Database.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 9, Part II of the report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation of the mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all department/offices of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Mainstreaming of GAD activities was already done.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipality thru its GAD Focal Point System fill-in appropriately all the columns in the GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report to sufficiently present the essential information to facilitate the review and evaluation of the same. Cause also the review/approval of the GAD Plan and Budget by the DILG Regional Director as required.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point Person complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person thru its GAD Focal Point System to plan and implement projects and programs that will directly benefit women. Consider the potentials and borne skills of women in the formulation of strategies and bases that will be used in designing priority projects and programs for GAD Plan responsive to its prescribed thrust and vision. We further reiterated that the GAD Focal Point Team fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget. In addition, strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the plan is fully implemented and the intent of GAD on promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment as mandated is fully met.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality included in its GAD Plan and Budget many gender responsive programs and projects. Most program and projects were implemented by the GAD Focal Point Team.
